questions.question: What problem did BERT solve for 2018-era NLP?
options: Every task trained its own model from scratch on its own labels — BERT pretrained a bidirectional encoder on all internet text to predict missing words, then fine-tuned one head per downstream task; within 18 months its family dominated every NLP leaderboard
questions.explanation: The "pretrain once, fine-tune everywhere" pattern was the revelation. By 2020 every search engine and moderation pipeline had a BERT inside.

questions.question: What can masked language modeling do that causal LM cannot?
options: Use context from BOTH sides: predicting the mask in "the [MASK] brown fox" can read "brown fox jumps" to the right — bidirectional attention that a causal model's triangle mask forbids
questions.explanation: GPT predicts the next word; BERT predicts a missing word. One sentence of difference — and half a decade of everything embedding-shaped.

questions.question: Why aren't all 15% of selected tokens replaced with [MASK]?
options: [MASK] never appears at inference — 100% masking would create a pretrain/fine-tune distribution shift, so 80% become [MASK], 10% become a random token, and 10% stay unchanged to keep the model honest
questions.explanation: The 10% unchanged forces the model to maintain good representations for every token, not just masked ones, since any position might be a prediction target.

questions.question: What happened to Next Sentence Prediction?
options: It remains essential in all encoders
RoBERTa (2019) ablated it and showed NSP HURT rather than helped — modern encoders skip it entirely
It was renamed to contrastive loss
It became the main pretraining task
questions.explanation: A cautionary tale about auxiliary objectives: plausible-sounding extra tasks need ablation, and BERT's own follow-up removed half its recipe.

questions.question: Why do encoder-only models still matter in 2026?
options: For classification, retrieval, and structured extraction they run 5-10x faster per token than decoders, and their embeddings back every modern retrieval stack — ModernBERT (Dec 2024) rebuilt the architecture with Flash Attention, RoPE, and GeGLU at 8K context
questions.explanation: Bidirectional encoding remains the right inductive bias when you need one vector per text rather than a continuation — generation isn't every task.
